expect: PHIL-8 DROP criterion can be tested with growth metric data — predict at least one 3+ consecutive decrease window exists in proxy-K/lesson-count/tool-count history
actual: First DROP criterion narrowly survives (max 2 consecutive decreases, not 3). Second DROP criterion partially met: for quality, attention-only model predicts equally well (Sharpe delta=0.00, n=177). PHIL-8 is mislabeled, not droppable.
diff: Expected at least one 3+ decrease window — found max 2. But discovered stronger finding: quality is INVARIANT to compaction (Sharpe delta=0.00).
hypothesis: PHIL-8 'Evolve through distillation' can be dropped — compaction doesn't cause quality evolution

method.test_1: Scanned 76 proxy-K entries for 3+ consecutive decreases without compact.py
method.test_2: Compared Sharpe in compaction-adjacent (n=110) vs non-adjacent (n=67) windows
method.test_3: Compared lesson output rate: early 2.46 vs late 5.18 L/session (+110%)

results.proxy_k_compaction_removal: 44.4% of positive growth removed by compaction

verdict: PARTIAL — PHIL-8 title mislabeled (evolve→compress). Quality unchanged by compaction. Not droppable by first criterion. Second criterion partially met for quality metrics.
recommendation: REVISE PHIL-8: rename from 'Evolve through distillation' to 'Compress through distillation'. Add challenge.
